`Bead` (including its workspace path) so the caller\n216\t    /// can create the correct bead store for remote beads found by Explore.\n217\t    /// The accompanying `String` is the name of the strand that produced the\n218\t    /// candidate.\n219\t    ///\n220\t    /// When a strand returns `WorkCreated`, the waterfall restarts from Pluck.\n221\t    /// A restart cap prevents infinite loops (e.g. a strand that always creates\n222\t    /// work without producing a claimable bead).\n223\t    pub async fn select(&self, store: &dyn BeadStore, exclusions: &HashSet<BeadId>) -> Result<SelectOutcome> {\n224\t        const MAX_RESTARTS: u32 = 3;\n225\t        let mut restarts = 0u32;\n226\t        let mut restart_triggers: Vec<String> = Vec::new();\n227\t\n228\t        'waterfall: loop {\n229\t            for strand in &self.strands {\n230\t                let start = Instant::now();\n231\t                let result = strand.evaluate(store).await;\n232\t                let elapsed_ms = start.elapsed().as_millis() as u64;\n233\t\n234\t                match result {\n235\t                    StrandResult::BeadFound(beads) => {\n236\t                        // Filter out beads that are in the exclusion set (e.g.\n237\t                        // recently race-lost).        let filtered: Vec<Bead> = beads\n241\t                            .into_iter()\n242\t                            .filter(|b| !exclusions.contains(&b.id))\n243\t                            .collect();\n244\t                        let excluded_count = beads.len().saturating_sub(filtered.len());\n245\t                        let _ = self\n246\t                            .telemetry\n247\t                            .emit(crate::telemetry::EventKind::StrandEvaluated {\n248\t                                strand_name: strand.name().to_string(),\n249\t                                result: \"bead_found\".to_string(),\n250\t                                duration_ms: elapsed_ms,\n251\t                            });\n252\t                        tracing::info!(\n253\t                            strand = strand.name(),\n254\t                            candidates = filtered.len(),\n255\t                            excluded = excluded_count,\n256\t                            elapsed_ms,\n257\t                            \"strand found candidates\"\n258\t                        );\n259\t                        if let Some(bead) = filtered.into_iter().next() {\n260\t                            return Ok(SelectOutcome {\n261\t                                bead: Some((bead, strand.name().to_string())),\n262\t                                waterfall_restarts: restarts,\n263\t                                restart_triggers,\n264\t                            });\n265\t                        }\n266\t                        continue;\n267\t                    }\n268\t                    StrandResult::WorkCreated => {\n269\t                        let _ = self\n270\t                            .telemetry\n271\t                            .emit(crate::telemetry::EventKind::StrandEvaluated {\n272\t                                strand_name: strand.name().to_string(),\n273\t                                result: \"work_created\".to_string(),\n274\t                                duration_ms: elapsed_ms,\n275\t                            });\n276\t                        restarts += 1;\n277\t                        restart_triggers.push(strand.name().to_string());\n278\t                        if restarts > MAX_RESTARTS {\n279\t                            tracing::warn!(\n280\t                                strand = strand.name(),\n281\t                                max_restarts = MAX_RESTARTS,\n282\t                                \"waterfall restart cap reached, continuing to evaluate remaining strands\"\n283\t                            );\n284\t                            // Do NOT return None — continue evaluating remaining strands\n285\t                            // so every strand emits telemetry and the operator can see\n286\t                            // why the worker is idle.\n287\t                            continue;\n288\t                        }\n289\t                        tracing::info!(\n290\t                            strand = strand.name(),\n291\t                            elapsed_ms,\n292\t                            restart = restarts,\n293\t                            \"strand created new work, restarting waterfall\"\n294\t                        );\n295\t                        continue 'waterfall;\n296\t                    }\n297\t                    StrandResult::NoWork => {\n298\t                        let _ = self\n299\t                            .telemetry\n300\t                            .emit(crate::telemetry::EventKind::StrandEvaluated {\n301\t                                strand_name: strand.name().to_string(),\n302\t                                result: \"no_work\".to_string(),\n303\t                                duration_ms: elapsed_ms,\n304\t                            });\n305\t                        tracing::info!(\n306\t                            strand = strand.name(),\n307\t                            elapsed_ms,\n308\t                            \"strand returned no work\"\n309\t                        );\n310\t                        continue;\n311\t                    }\n312\t                    StrandResult::Error(e) => {\n313\t                        let _ = self\n314\t                            .telemetry\n315\t                            .emit(crate::telemetry::EventKind::StrandEvaluated {\n316\t                                strand_name: strand.name().to_string(),\n317\t                                result: \"error\".to_string(),\n318\t                                duration_ms: elapsed_ms,\n319\t                            });\n320\t                        tracing::warn!(\n321\t strand\"\n325\t                        );\n326\t                        continue;\n327\t                    }\n328\t                }\n329\t            }\n330\t            // All strands evaluated without finding work or triggering a restart.\n331\t            return Ok(SelectOutcome {\n332\t                bead: None,\n333\t                waterfall_restarts: restarts,\n334\t                restart_triggers,\n335\t            });\n336\t        }\n337\t    }\n338\t\n339\t    /// Return the names of all configured strands (for telemetry/debugging).\n340\t    pub fn strand_names(&self) -> }\n590\t\n591\t    impl CountingStrand {\n592\t        fn no_work(\n593\t            name: &'static str,\n594\t            count: std::sync::Arc<std::sync::atomic::AtomicU32>,\n595\t        ) -> Self {\n596\t            CountingStrand {\n597\t                name,\n598\t                count,\n599\t                returns_work_created: false,\n600\t            }\n601\t        }\n602\t\n603\t        fn work_created(\n604\t            name: &'static str,\n605\t            count: std::sync::Arc<std::sync::atomic::AtomicU32>,\n606\t        ) -> Self {\n607\t            CountingStrand {\n608\t                name,\n609\t                count,\n610\t                returns_work_created: true,\n611\t            }\n612\t        }\n613\t    }\n614\t\n615\t    #[async_trait::async_trait]\n616\t StrandResult {\n622\t            self.count.fetch_add(1, std::sync::atomic::Ordering::SeqCst);\n623\t            if self.returns_work_created {\n624\t                StrandResult::WorkCreated\n625\t            } else {\n626\t                StrandResult::NoWork\n627\t            }\n628\t        }\n629\t    }\n630\t\n631\t    #[tokio::test]\n632\t    async fn restart_cap_still_evaluates_remaining_strands() {\n633\t        // When a strand repeatedly returns WorkCreated, the waterfall restarts.\n634\t        // After MAX_RESTARTS, the remaining strands should still be evaluated\n635\t        // so that operators see telemetry for every strand in the cycle.\n636\t        let creator_count = std::sync::Arc::new(std::sync::atomic::AtomicU32::new(0));\n637\t        let observer_a_count = std::sync::Arc::new(std::sync::atomic::AtomicU32::new(0));\n638\t        let observer_b_count = std::sync::Arc::new(std::sync::atomic::AtomicU32::new(0));\n639\t\n640\t        let runner = StrandRunner::new(vec![\n641\t            Box::new(CountingStrand::work_created(\n642\t                \"creator\",\n643\t                creator_count.clone(),\n644\t            )),\n645\t            Box::new(CountingStrand::no_work(\n646\t                \"observer-a\",\n647\t                observer_a_count.clone(),\n648\t            )),\n649\t            Box::new(CountingStrand::no_work(\n650\t                \"observer-b\",\n651\t                observer_b_count.clone(),\n652\t            )),\n653\t        ]);\n654\t        let store = EmptyStore;\n655\t        let outcome = runner.select(&store).await.unwrap();\n656\t        assert!(outcome.bead.is_none());\n657\t\n658\t        // creator should have been evaluated MAX_RESTARTS + 1 = 4 times.\n659\t        assert_eq!(creator_count.load(std::sync::atomic::Ordering::SeqCst), 4);\n660\t\n661\t        // After the restart cap, the remaining strands should each be evaluated\n662\t        // at least once (the final pass through the waterfall).\n663\t        let a = observer_a_count.load(std::sync::atomic::Ordering::SeqCst);\n664\t        let b = observer_b_count.load(std::sync::atomic::Ordering::SeqCst);\n665\t        assert!(\n666\t            a >= 1,\n667\t            \"observer_a should be evaluated at least once, got {a}\"\n668\t        );\n669\t        assert!(\n670\t            b >= 1,\n671\t            \"observer_b should be evaluated at least once, got {b}\"\n672\t        );\n673\t\n674\t        // All 4 WorkCreated events are captured in restart_triggers.\n675\t        assert_eq!(outcome.waterfall_restarts, 4);\n676\t        assert_eq!(outcome.restart_triggers.len(), 4);\n677\t        assert!(outcome.restart_triggers.iter().all(|t| t == \"creator\"));\n678\t    }\n679\t\n680\t    #[tokio::test]\n681\t    async fn       );\n693\t    }\n694\t\n695\t    #[tokio::test]\n696\t    async fn multiple_beads_returns_first() {\n697\t        let bead1 = make_test_bead(\"first\");\n698\t        let bead2 = Strand waterfall: ordered sequence of selection strategies.\n//!\n//! The StrandRunner evaluates strands in priority order. The first strand\n//! that yields a candidate wins. Strands are stateless — they receive queue\n//! state and return a candidate or nothing.\n//!\n//! Depends on: `types`, `config`, `bead_store`.\n\nmod explore;\nmod knot;\nmod mend;\nmod pluck;\npub mod pulse;\npub mod reflect;\npub mod splice;\npub mod unravel;\npub mod weave;\n\nuse std::collections::HashSet;\nuse std::time::Instant;\n\nuse anyhow::Result;\n\nuse crate::bead_store::BeadStore;\nuse crate::config::Config;\nuse crate::types::{Bead, BeadId, StrandResult};\n\n/// Result of a single `StrandRunner::select()` call.\n///\n/// Carries both the winning candidate (if any) and diagnostic statistics\n/// about restarts that occurred during the waterfall — used to populate\n/// `worker.exhausted` telemetry with a per-iteration breakdown.\n#[derive(Debug, Default)]\npub struct SelectOutcome {\n    /// The candidate bead and the strand that found it, or `None` if\n    /// all strands returned `NoWork`.\n    pub bead: Option<(Bead, String)>,\n    /// How many times the waterfall restarted from Pluck (cap = `MAX_RESTARTS`).\n    pub waterfall_restarts: u32,\n    /// Names of strands that returned `WorkCreated` and triggered a restart.\n    /// Duplicate entries are preserved (one per restart event).\n    pub restart_triggers: Vec<String>,\n}\n\npub use explore::ExploreStrand;\npub use knot::KnotStrand;\npub use mend::{cleanup_orphaned_in_progress, MendStrand};\npub use pluck::PluckStrand;\npub use pulse::PulseStrand;\npub use reflect::{CliReflectAgent, ReflectAgent, ReflectStrand};\npub use splice::SpliceStrand;\npub use unravel::{UnravelAgent, UnravelStrand};\npub use weave::{CliWeaveAgent, WeaveAgent, WeaveStrand};\n\n/// A single selection strategy in the waterfall.\n#[async_trait::async_trait]\npub trait Strand: Send + Sync {\n    /// Human-readable name for telemetry.\n    fn name(&self) -> &str;\n\n    /// Evaluate this strand against the current queue state.\n    async fn evaluate(&self, store: &dyn BeadStore) -> StrandResult;\n}\n\n/// Runs strands in order, returning the first candidate found.\npub struct StrandRunner {\n    strands: Vec<Box<dyn Strand>>,\n    telemetry: crate::telemetry::Telemetry,\n}\n\nimpl StrandRunner {\n    pub fn new(strands: Vec<Box<dyn Strand>>) -> Self {\n        StrandRunner {\n            strands,\n            telemetry: crate::telemetry::Telemetry::new(\"strand-runner\".to_string()),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Build the default strand waterfall from config.\n    ///\n    /// The waterfall order is:\n    /// Pluck → Mend → Explore → Weave → Unravel → Pulse → Reflect → Splice → Knot.\n    pub fn from_config(\n        config: &Config,\n        worker_id: &str,\n        registry: crate::registry::Registry,\n        telemetry: crate::telemetry::Telemetry,\n    ) -> Self {\n        let pluck = PluckStrand::new(\n            config.strands.pluck.exclude_labels.clone(),\n            telemetry.clone(),\n        );\n\n        let heartbeat_dir = config.workspace.home.join(\"state\").join(\"heartbeats\");\n        let heartbeat_ttl = std::time::Duration::from_secs(config.health.heartbeat_ttl_secs);\n        let lock_dir = std::path::PathBuf::from(\"/tmp\");\n        let log_dir = config\n            .telemetry\n            .file_sink\n            .log_dir\n            .clone()\n            .unwrap_or_else(|| config.workspace.home.join(\"logs\"));\n        let retention_days = config.telemetry.file_sink.retention_days;\n        let traces_dir = config.workspace.default.join(\".beads\").join(\"traces\");\n        let trace_retention_failed_days = config.strands.learning.trace_retention_failed_days;\n        let trace_retention_success_days = config.strands.learning.trace_retention_success_days;\n\n        // Create a new Registry instance pointing to the same path for ExploreStrand.\n        // We need to get the state_dir_for_explore before moving registry to MendStrand.\n        let state_fallback = config.workspace.home.join(\"state\");\n        let state_dir_for_explore = registry.path().parent().unwrap_or(&state_fallback);\n        let explore_registry = crate::registry::Registry::new(state_dir_for_explore);\n\n        let mend = MendStrand::new(\n            config.strands.mend.clone(),\n            heartbeat_dir,\n            heartbeat_ttl,\n            lock_dir,\n            worker_id.to_string(),\n            registry,\n            telemetry.clone(),\n            log_dir,\n            retention_days,\n            traces_dir,\n            trace_retention_failed_days,\n            trace_retention_success_days,\n            config.workspace.default.clone(),\n            config.strands.learning.max_learnings,\n        );\n\n        let explore = ExploreStrand::new(\n            config.strands.explore.clone(),\n            config.workspace.default.clone(),\n            explore_registry,\n            telemetry.clone(),\n            worker_id.to_string(),\n        );\n\n        let state_base = config.workspace.home.join(\"state\");\n\n        let weave = WeaveStrand::new(\n            config.strands.weave.clone(),\n            config.workspace.default.clone(),\n            state_base.join(\"weave\"),\n            Box::new(CliWeaveAgent::new(config.agent.default.clone())),\n            telemetry.clone(),\n        );\n\n        let unravel = UnravelStrand::new(\n            config.strands.unravel.clone(),\n            config.workspace.default.clone(),\n            state_base.join(\"unravel\"),\n            Box::new(unravel::CliUnravelAgent::new(config.agent.default.clone())),\n            telemetry.clone(),\n        );\n\n        let pulse = PulseStrand::new(\n            config.strands.pulse.clone(),\n            config.workspace.default.clone(),\n            state_base.join(\"pulse\"),\n            telemetry.clone(),\n        );\n\n        // Create the extraction agent if configured.\n        let reflect_agent = config\n            .strands\n            .reflect\n            .extraction_agent\n            .as_ref()\n            .map(|agent_cmd| {\n                Box::new(reflect::CliReflectAgent::new(\n                    agent_cmd.clone(),\n                    config.strands.reflect.extraction_prompt_template.clone(),\n                )) as Box<dyn reflect::ReflectAgent>\n            });\n\n        let reflect = ReflectStrand::new(\n            config.strands.reflect.clone(),\n            config.workspace.default.clone(),\n            state_base.join(\"reflect\"),\n            telemetry.clone(),\n            reflect_agent,\n        );\n\n        // Reconstruct heartbeat_dir for Splice (same path used by Mend).\n        let splice_heartbeat_dir = config.workspace.home.join(\"state\").join(\"heartbeats\");\n        let runner_telemetry = telemetry.clone();\n        let splice = SpliceStrand::new(\n            config.strands.splice.clone(),\n            splice_heartbeat_dir,\n            state_base.join(\"splice\"),\n            telemetry,\n        );\n\n        let knot = KnotStrand::new(config.strands.knot.clone(), runner_telemetry.clone());\n        StrandRunner {\n            strands: vec![\n                Box::new(pluck),\n                Box::new(mend),\n                Box::new(explore),\n                Box::new(weave),\n                Box::new(unravel),\n                Box::new(pulse),\n                Box::new(reflect),\n                Box::new(splice),\n                Box::new(knot),\n            ],\n            telemetry: runner_telemetry,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Run the waterfall, returning a `SelectOutcome` that carries the winning\n    /// candidate (if any) plus restart diagnostics.\n    ///\n    /// Returns the full `Bead` (including its workspace path) so the caller\n    /// can create the correct bead store for remote beads found by Explore.\n    /// The accompanying `String` is the name of the strand that produced the\n    /// candidate.\n    ///\n    /// When a strand returns `WorkCreated`, the waterfall restarts from Pluck.\n    /// A restart cap prevents infinite loops (e.g. a strand that always creates\n    /// work without producing a claimable bead).\n    pub async fn select(&self, store: &dyn BeadStore, exclusions: &HashSet<BeadId>) -> Result<SelectOutcome> {\n        const MAX_RESTARTS: u32 = 3;\n        let mut restarts = 0u32;\n        let mut restart_triggers: Vec<String> = Vec::new();\n\n        'waterfall: loop {\n            for strand in &self.strands {\n                let start = Instant::now();\n                let result = strand.evaluate(store).await;\n                let elapsed_ms = start.elapsed().as_millis() as u64;\n\n                match result {\n                    StrandResult::BeadFound(beads) => {\n                        // Filter out beads that are in the exclusion set (e.g.\n                        // recently race-lost).  This prevents the waterfall from\n                        // immediately re-selecting a bead that just lost a claim\n                        // race to another worker.\n                        let filtered: Vec<Bead> = beads\n                            .into_iter()\n                            .filter(|b| !exclusions.contains(&b.id))\n                            .collect();\n                        let excluded_count = beads.len().saturating_sub(filtered.len());\n                        let _ = self\n                            .telemetry\n                            .emit(crate::telemetry::EventKind::StrandEvaluated {\n                                strand_name: strand.name().to_string(),\n                                result: \"bead_found\".to_string(),\n                                duration_ms: elapsed_ms,\n                            });\n                        tracing::info!(\n                            strand = strand.name(),\n                            candidates = filtered.len(),\n                            excluded = excluded_count,\n                            elapsed_ms,\n                            \"strand found candidates\"\n                        );\n                        if let Some(bead) = filtered.into_iter().next() {\n                            return Ok(SelectOutcome {\n                                bead: Some((bead, strand.name().to_string())),\n                                waterfall_restarts: restarts,\n                                restart_triggers,\n                            });\n                        }\n                        continue;\n                    }\n                    StrandResult::WorkCreated => {\n                        let _ = self\n                            .telemetry\n                            .emit(crate::telemetry::EventKind::StrandEvaluated {\n                                strand_name: strand.name().to_string(),\n                                result: \"work_created\".to_string(),\n                                duration_ms: elapsed_ms,\n                            });\n                        restarts += 1;\n                        restart_triggers.push(strand.name().to_string());\n                        if restarts > MAX_RESTARTS {\n                            tracing::warn!(\n                                strand = strand.name(),\n                                max_restarts = MAX_RESTARTS,\n                                \"waterfall restart cap reached, continuing to evaluate remaining strands\"\n                            );\n                            // Do NOT return None — continue evaluating remaining strands\n                            // so every strand emits telemetry and the operator can see\n                            // why the worker is idle.\n                            continue;\n                        }\n                        tracing::info!(\n                            strand = strand.name(),\n                            elapsed_ms,\n                            restart = restarts,\n                            \"strand created new work, restarting waterfall\"\n                        );\n                        continue 'waterfall;\n                    }\n                    StrandResult::NoWork => {\n                        let _ = self\n                            .telemetry\n                            .emit(crate::telemetry::EventKind::StrandEvaluated {\n                                strand_name: strand.name().to_string(),\n                                result: \"no_work\".to_string(),\n                                duration_ms: elapsed_ms,\n                            });\n                        tracing::info!(\n                            strand = strand.name(),\n                            elapsed_ms,\n                            \"strand returned no work\"\n                        );\n                        continue;\n                    }\n                    StrandResult::Error(e) => {\n                        let _ = self\n                            .telemetry\n                            .emit(crate::telemetry::EventKind::StrandEvaluated {\n                                strand_name: strand.name().to_string(),\n                                result: \"error\".to_string(),\n                                duration_ms: elapsed_ms,\n                            });\n                        tracing::warn!(\n                            strand = strand.name(),\n                            error = %e,\n                            elapsed_ms,\n                            \"strand error, continuing to next strand\"\n                        );\n                        continue;\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n            // All strands evaluated without finding work or triggering a restart.\n            return Ok(SelectOutcome {\n                bead: None,\n                waterfall_restarts: restarts,\n                restart_triggers,\n            });\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Return the names of all configured strands (for telemetry/debugging).\n    pub fn strand_names(&self) -> Vec<&str> {\n        self.strands.iter().map(|s| s.name()).collect()\n    }\n}\n\n// ─── Unit tests ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n    use crate::types::{Bead, BeadId};\n\n    /// A stub strand that always returns the given result.\n    struct StubStrand {\n        name: &'static str,\n        result: std::sync::Mutex<Option<StrandResult>>,\n    }\n\n    impl StubStrand {\n        fn no_work(name: &'static str) -> Self {\n            StubStrand {\n                name,\n                result: std::sync::Mutex::new(Some(StrandResult::NoWork)),\n            }\n        }\n\n        fn beads(name: &'static str, beads: Vec<Bead>) -> Self {\n            StubStrand {\n                name,\n                result: std::sync::Mutex::new(Some(StrandResult::BeadFound(beads))),\n            }\n        }\n\n        fn work_created(name: &'static str) -> Self {\n            StubStrand {\n                name,\n                result: std::sync::Mutex::new(Some(StrandResult::WorkCreated)),\n            }\n        }\n\n        fn error(name: &'static str, msg: &str) -> Self {\n            StubStrand {\n                name,\n                result: std::sync::Mutex::new(Some(StrandResult::Error(\n                    crate::types::StrandError::ConfigError(msg.to_string()),\n                ))),\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[async_trait::async_trait]\n    impl Strand for StubStrand {\n        fn name(&self) -> &str {\n            self.name\n        }\n\n        async fn evaluate(&self, _store: &dyn BeadStore) -> StrandResult {\n            self.result\n                .lock()\n                .unwrap()\n                .take()\n                .unwrap_or(StrandResult::NoWork)\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn make_test_bead(id: &str) -> Bead {\n        use chrono::Utc;\n        Bead {\n            id: BeadId::from(id.to_string()),\n            title: format!(\"Test bead {id}\"),\n            body: None,\n            priority: 1,\n            status: crate::types::BeadStatus::Open,\n            assignee: None,\n            labels: vec![],\n            workspace: std::path::PathBuf::from(\"/tmp/test\"),\n            dependencies: vec![],\n            dependents: vec![],\n            created_at: Utc::now(),\n            updated_at: Utc::now(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Stub BeadStore for tests — always returns empty.\n    struct EmptyStore;\n\n    #[async_trait::async_trait]\n    impl BeadStore for EmptyStore {\n        async fn list_all(&self) -> Result<Vec<Bead>> {\n            Ok(vec![])\n        }\n        async fn ready(&self, _filters: &crate::bead_store::Filters) -> Result<Vec<Bead>> {\n            Ok(vec![])\n        }\n        async fn show(&self, _id: &BeadId) -> Result<Bead> {\n            anyhow::bail!(\"not found\")\n        }\n        async fn claim(&self, _id: &BeadId, _actor: &str) -> Result<crate::types::ClaimResult> {\n            anyhow::bail!(\"not implemented\")\n        }\n        async fn release(&self, _id: &BeadId) -> Result<()> {\n            Ok(())\n        }\n        async fn flush(&self) -> Result<()> {\n            Ok(())\n        }\n        async fn reopen(&self, _id: &BeadId) -> Result<()> {\n            Ok(())\n        }\n        async fn labels(&self, _id: &BeadId) -> Result<Vec<String>> {\n            Ok(vec![])\n        }\n        async fn add_label(&self, _id: &BeadId, _label: &str) -> Result<()> {\n            Ok(())\n        }\n        async fn remove_label(&self, _id: &BeadId, _label: &str) -> Result<()> {\n            Ok(())\n        }\n        async fn create_bead(&self, _title: &str, _body: &str, _labels: &[&str]) -> Result<BeadId> {\n            Ok(BeadId::from(\"new-bead\".to_string()))\n        }\n        async fn doctor_repair(&self) -> Result<crate::bead_store::RepairReport> {\n            Ok(crate::bead_store::RepairReport::default())\n        }\n        async fn doctor_check(&self) -> Result<crate::bead_store::RepairReport> {\n            Ok(crate::bead_store::RepairReport::default())\n        }\n        async fn full_rebuild(&self) -> Result<()> {\n            Ok(())\n        }\n        async fn add_dependency(&self, _blocker_id: &BeadId, _blocked_id: &BeadId) -> Result<()> {\n            Ok(())\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn empty_waterfall_returns_none() {\n        let runner = StrandRunner::new(vec![]);\n        let store = EmptyStore;\n        let outcome = runner.select(&store, &HashSet::new()).await.unwrap();\n        assert!(outcome.bead.is_none());\n        assert_eq!(outcome.waterfall_restarts, 0);\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn first_strand_with_beads_wins() {\n        let bead = make_test_bead(\"test-001\");\n        let runner = StrandRunner::new(vec![\n            Box::new(StubStrand::no_work(\"empty\")),\n            Box::new(StubStrand::beads(\"finder\", vec![bead])),\n        ]);\n        let store = EmptyStore;\n        let outcome = runner.select(&store, &HashSet::new()).await.unwrap();\n        let (bead, strand_name) = outcome.bead.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(bead.id, BeadId::from(\"test-001\".to_string()));\n        assert_eq!(strand_name, \"finder\");\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn work_created_restarts_waterfall() {\n        let runner = StrandRunner::new(vec![\n            Box::new(StubStrand::work_created(\"creator\")),\n            Box::new(StubStrand::beads(\n                \"finder\",\n                vec![make_test_bead(\"test-002\")],\n            )),\n        ]);\n        let store = EmptyStore;\n        // WorkCreated restarts the waterfall. On the second pass, \"creator\"\n        // returns NoWork (stub consumed) and \"finder\" yields the bead.\n        let outcome = runner.select(&store, &HashSet::new()).await.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(\n            outcome.bead.map(|(b, _)| b.id),\n            Some(BeadId::from(\"test-002\".to_string()))\n        );\n        assert_eq!(outcome.waterfall_restarts, 1);\n        assert_eq!(outcome.restart_triggers, vec![\"creator\"]);\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn all_no_work_returns_none() {\n        let runner = StrandRunner::new(vec![\n            Box::new(StubStrand::no_work(\"s1\")),\n            Box::new(StubStrand::no_work(\"s2\")),\n            Box::new(StubStrand::no_work(\"s3\")),\n        ]);\n        let store = EmptyStore;\n        let outcome = runner.select(&store, &HashSet::new()).await.unwrap();\n        assert!(outcome.bead.is_none());\n        assert_eq!(outcome.waterfall_restarts, 0);\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn strand_names_returns_all_names() {\n        let runner = StrandRunner::new(vec![\n            Box::new(StubStrand::no_work(\"alpha\")),\n            Box::new(StubStrand::no_work(\"beta\")),\n        ]);\n        assert_eq!(runner.strand_names(), vec![\"alpha\", \"beta\"]);\n    }\n\n    /// A strand that always returns WorkCreated (never consumed).\n    struct AlwaysWorkCreated;\n\n    #[async_trait::async_trait]\n    impl Strand for AlwaysWorkCreated {\n        fn name(&self) -> &str {\n            \"always-creates\"\n        }\n        async fn evaluate(&self, _store: &dyn BeadStore) -> StrandResult {\n            StrandResult::WorkCreated\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn error_strand_continues_to_next() {\n        let bead = make_test_bead(\"after-error\");\n        let runner = StrandRunner::new(vec![\n            Box::new(StubStrand::error(\"broken\", \"something went wrong\")),\n            Box::new(StubStrand::beads(\"finder\", vec![bead])),\n        ]);\n        let store = EmptyStore;\n        let outcome = runner.select(&store, &HashSet::new()).await.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(\n            outcome.bead.map(|(b, _)| b.id),\n            Some(BeadId::from(\"after-error\".to_string()))\n        );\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn restart_cap_prevents_infinite_loop() {\n        // AlwaysWorkCreated triggers restarts every pass.\n        // After MAX_RESTARTS (3), the waterfall should return None.\n        let runner = StrandRunner::new(vec![Box::new(AlwaysWorkCreated)]);\n        let store = EmptyStore;\n        let outcome = runner.select(&store).await.unwrap();\n        assert!(outcome.bead.is_none());\n        assert_eq!(outcome.waterfall_restarts, 4); // 3 restarts + 1 cap-exceeded\n        assert_eq!(\n            outcome.restart_triggers,\n            vec![\"always-creates\", \"always-creates\", \"always-creates\", \"always-creates\"]\n        );\n    }\n\n    /// Strand that increments an external counter each time it is evaluated.\n    struct CountingStrand {\n        name: &'static str,\n        count: std::sync::Arc<std::sync::atomic::AtomicU32>,\n        returns_work_created: bool,\n    }\n\n    impl CountingStrand {\n        fn no_work(\n            name: &'static str,\n            count: std::sync::Arc<std::sync::atomic::AtomicU32>,\n        ) -> Self {\n            CountingStrand {\n                name,\n                count,\n                returns_work_created: false,\n            }\n        }\n\n        fn work_created(\n            name: &'static str,\n            count: std::sync::Arc<std::sync::atomic::AtomicU32>,\n        ) -> Self {\n            CountingStrand {\n                name,\n                count,\n                returns_work_created: true,\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[async_trait::async_trait]\n    impl Strand for CountingStrand {\n        fn name(&self) -> &str {\n            self.name\n        }\n\n        async fn evaluate(&self, _store: &dyn BeadStore) -> StrandResult {\n            self.count.fetch_add(1, std::sync::atomic::Ordering::SeqCst);\n            if self.returns_work_created {\n                StrandResult::WorkCreated\n            } else {\n                StrandResult::NoWork\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn restart_cap_still_evaluates_remaining_strands() {\n        // When a strand repeatedly returns WorkCreated, the waterfall restarts.\n        // After MAX_RESTARTS, the remaining strands should still be evaluated\n        // so that operators see telemetry for every strand in the cycle.\n        let creator_count = std::sync::Arc::new(std::sync::atomic::AtomicU32::new(0));\n        let observer_a_count = std::sync::Arc::new(std::sync::atomic::AtomicU32::new(0));\n        let observer_b_count = std::sync::Arc::new(std::sync::atomic::AtomicU32::new(0));\n\n        let runner = StrandRunner::new(vec![\n            Box::new(CountingStrand::work_created(\n                \"creator\",\n                creator_count.clone(),\n            )),\n            Box::new(CountingStrand::no_work(\n                \"observer-a\",\n                observer_a_count.clone(),\n            )),\n            Box::new(CountingStrand::no_work(\n                \"observer-b\",\n                observer_b_count.clone(),\n            )),\n        ]);\n        let store = EmptyStore;\n        let outcome = runner.select(&store).await.unwrap();\n        assert!(outcome.bead.is_none());\n\n        // creator should have been evaluated MAX_RESTARTS + 1 = 4 times.\n        assert_eq!(creator_count.load(std::sync::atomic::Ordering::SeqCst), 4);\n\n        // After the restart cap, the remaining strands should each be evaluated\n        // at least once (the final pass through the waterfall).\n        let a = observer_a_count.load(std::sync::atomic::Ordering::SeqCst);\n        let b = observer_b_count.load(std::sync::atomic::Ordering::SeqCst);\n        assert!(\n            a >= 1,\n            \"observer_a should be evaluated at least once, got {a}\"\n        );\n        assert!(\n            b >= 1,\n            \"observer_b should be evaluated at least once, got {b}\"\n        );\n\n        // All 4 WorkCreated events are captured in restart_triggers.\n        assert_eq!(outcome.waterfall_restarts, 4);\n        assert_eq!(outcome.restart_triggers.len(), 4);\n        assert!(outcome.restart_triggers.iter().all(|t| t == \"creator\"));\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn empty_bead_found_continues_to_next() {\n        let bead = make_test_bead(\"real-bead\");\n        let runner = StrandRunner::new(vec![\n            Box::new(StubStrand::beads(\"empty-finder\", vec![])),\n            Box::new(StubStrand::beads(\"real-finder\", vec![bead])),\n        ]);\n        let store = EmptyStore;\n        let outcome = runner.select(&store).await.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(\n            outcome.bead.map(|(b, _)| b.id),\n            Some(BeadId::from(\"real-bead\".to_string()))\n        );\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn multiple_beads_returns_first() {\n        let bead1 = make_test_bead(\"first\");\n        let bead2 = make_test_bead(\"second\");\n        let runner = StrandRunner::new(vec![Box::new(StubStrand::beads(\n            \"multi\",\n            vec![bead1, bead2],\n        ))]);\n        let store = EmptyStore;\n        let outcome = runner.select(&store).await.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(\n            outcome.bead.map(|(b, _)| b.id),\n            Some(BeadId::from(\"first\".to_string()))\n        );\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn from_config_includes_full_waterfall() {\n        let dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n        let StrandRunner::from_config(&config, \"test-worker\", registry, telemetry);\n        assert_eq!(\n            runner.strand_names(),\n            vec![\n                \"pluck\", \"mend\", \"explore\", configured, nothing to explore.\n        if !self.enabled {\n            let _ = self\n                .telemetry\n                .emit(crate::telemetry::EventKind::StrandSkipped {\n \"explore\".to_string(),\n                    reason: \"no_workspaces_configured\".to_string(),\n                });\n            return StrandResult::NoWork;\n        }\n\n        let filters = {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_e50d0dc658ec4d48970a77b2","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t//! Unravel strand: propose automated alternatives for human-blocked beads.\n2\t//!\n3\t//! When beads carry the \"human\" label (requiring a human decision before\n4\t//! proceeding), Unravel dispatches an AI agent to propose workarounds that\n5\t//! could be executed autonomously. Alternative proposals are created as\n6\t//! child beads of the original human-labeled bead.\n7\t//!\n8\t//! **Guardrails:**\n9\t//! - Opt-in only (disabled by default).\n10\t//! - Max beads analyzed per run.\n11\t//! - Max alternatives per bead.\n12\t//! - 7-day cooldown per bead (already-analyzed beads are skipped).\n13\t//! - Original human bead is never modified or closed.\n14\t//!\n15\t//! Depends on: `bead_store`, `config`, `telemetry`, `types`.\n16\t\n17\tuse std::collections::HashMap;\n18\tuse std::path::{Path, PathBuf};\n19\t\n20\tuse anyhow::{Context, Result};\n21\tuse chrono::{DateTime, Utc};\n22\tuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\n23\tuse sha2::{Digest, Sha256};\n24\t\n25\tuse crate::bead_store::BeadStore;\n26\tuse crate::config::UnravelConfig;\n27\tuse crate::telemetry::{EventKind, Telemetry};\n28\tuse crate::types::{Bead, BeadId, StrandResult};\n29\t\n30\t// ─── UnravelAgent trait ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n31\t\n32\t/// Abstraction for agent invocation used by the Unravel strand.\n33\t///\n34\t/// Production implementations wrap the `Dispatcher`; tests use mocks.\n35\t#[async_trait::async_trait]\n36\tpub trait UnravelAgent: Send + Sync {\n37\t    /// Invoke an agent with the given prompt, returning its raw text response.\n38\t    async fn propose_alternatives(&self, prompt: &str, workspace: &Path) -> Result<String>;\n39\t}\n40\t\n41\t// ─── Proposed alternative (parsed from agent response) ──────────────────────\n42\t\n43\t/// An alternative approach proposed by the agent for a human-blocked bead.\n44\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n45\tpub struct ProposedAlternative {\n46\t    pub title: String,\n47\t    pub body: String,\n48\t}\n49\t\n50\t// ─── Persistent state ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n51\t\n52\t/// Persisted state for the Unravel strand.\n53\t///\n54\t/// Tracks which beads have been analyzed and when, enforcing the cooldown.\n55\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n56\tpub struct UnravelState {\n57\t    /// Map of bead ID -> last analysis timestamp.\n58\t    pub analyzed: HashMap<String, DateTime<Utc>>,\n59\t}\n60\t\n61\timpl UnravelState {\n62\t    /// Load state from disk, returning default if file doesn't exist.\n63\t    fn load(path: &Path) -> Result<Self> {\n64\t        match std::fs::read_to_string(path) {\n65\t            Ok(data) => serde_json::from_str(&data)\n66\t                .with_context(|| format!(\"failed to parse unravel state: {}\", path.display())),\n67\t            Err(e) if e.kind() == std::io::ErrorKind::NotFound => Ok(Self::default()),\n68\t            Err(e) => {\n69\t                Err(e).with_context(|| format!(\"failed to read unravel state: {}\", path.display()))\n70\t            }\n71\t        }\n72\t    }\n73\t\n74\t    /// Persist state to disk.\n75\t    fn save(&self, path: &Path) -> Result<()> {\n76\t        if let Some(parent) = path.parent() {\n77\t            std::fs::create_dir_all(parent)\n78\t                .with_context(|| format!(\"failed to create state dir: {}\", parent.display()))?;\n79\t        }\n80\t        let data =\n81\t            serde_json::to_string_pretty(self).context(\"failed to serialize unravel state\")?;\n82\t        std::fs::write(path, data)\n83\t            .with_context(|| format!(\"failed to write unravel state: {}\", path.display()))\n84\t    }\n85\t\n86\t    /// Check if a bead is within the cooldown period.\n87\t    fn is_in_cooldown(&self, bead_id: &BeadId, cooldown_hours: i64) -> Unravel strand: propose automated alternatives for human-blocked beads.\n//!\n//! When beads carry the \"human\" label (requiring a human decision before\n//! proceeding), Unravel dispatches an AI agent to propose workarounds that\n//! could be executed autonomously. Alternative proposals are created as\n//! child beads of the original human-labeled bead.\n//!\n//! **Guardrails:**\n//! - Opt-in only (disabled by default).\n//! - Max beads analyzed per run.\n//! - Max alternatives per bead.\n//! - 7-day cooldown per bead (already-analyzed beads are skipped).\n//! - Original human bead is never modified or closed.\n//!\n//! Depends on: `bead_store`, `config`, `telemetry`, `types`.\n\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\nuse std::path::{Path, PathBuf};\n\nuse anyhow::{Context, Result};\nuse chrono::{DateTime, Utc};\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse sha2::{Digest, Sha256};\n\nuse crate::bead_store::BeadStore;\nuse crate::config::UnravelConfig;\nuse crate::telemetry::{EventKind, Telemetry};\nuse crate::types::{Bead, BeadId, StrandResult};\n\n// ─── UnravelAgent trait ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Abstraction for agent invocation used by the Unravel strand.\n///\n/// Production implementations wrap the `Dispatcher`; tests use mocks.\n#[async_trait::async_trait]\npub trait UnravelAgent: Send + Sync {\n    /// Invoke an agent with the given prompt, returning its raw text response.\n    async fn propose_alternatives(&self, prompt: &str, workspace: &Path) -> Result<String>;\n}\n\n// ─── Proposed alternative (parsed from agent response) ──────────────────────\n\n/// An alternative approach proposed by the agent for a human-blocked bead.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct ProposedAlternative {\n    pub title: String,\n    pub body: String,\n}\n\n// ─── Persistent state ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Persisted state for the Unravel strand.\n///\n/// Tracks which beads have been analyzed and when, enforcing the cooldown.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct UnravelState {\n    /// Map of bead ID -> last analysis timestamp.\n    pub analyzed: HashMap<String, DateTime<Utc>>,\n}\n\nimpl UnravelState {\n    /// Load state from disk, returning default if file doesn't exist.\n    fn load(path: &Path) -> Result<Self> {\n        match std::fs::read_to_string(path) {\n            Ok(data) => serde_json::from_str(&data)\n                .with_context(|| format!(\"failed to parse unravel state: {}\", path.display())),\n            Err(e) if e.kind() == std::io::ErrorKind::NotFound => Ok(Self::default()),\n            Err(e) => {\n                Err(e).with_context(|| format!(\"failed to read unravel state: {}\", path.display()))\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Persist state to disk.\n    fn save(&self, path: &Path) -> Result<()> {\n        if let Some(parent) = path.parent() {\n            std::fs::create_dir_all(parent)\n                .with_context(|| format!(\"failed to create state dir: {}\", parent.display()))?;\n        }\n        let data =\n            serde_json::to_string_pretty(self).context(\"failed to serialize unravel state\")?;\n        std::fs::write(path, data)\n            .with_context(|| format!(\"failed to write unravel state: {}\", path.display()))\n    }\n\n    /// Check if a bead is within the cooldown period.\n    fn is_in_cooldown(&self, bead_id: &BeadId, cooldown_hours: i64) -> bool {\n        match self.analyzed.get(bead_id.as_ref()) {\n            None => false,\n            Some(last) => {\n                let elapsed = Utc::now().signed_duration_since(*last);\n                elapsed.num_hours() < cooldown_hours\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Record that a bead has been analyzed.\n    fn mark_analyzed(&mut self, bead_id: &BeadId) {\n        self.analyzed.insert(bead_id.to_string(), Utc::now());\n    }\n}\n\n// ─── UnravelStrand ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// The Unravel strand — proposes automated alternatives for human-blocked beads.\npub struct UnravelStrand {\n    config: UnravelConfig,\n    workspace: PathBuf,\n    state_dir: PathBuf,\n    agent: Box<dyn UnravelAgent>,\n    telemetry: Telemetry,\n}\n\nimpl UnravelStrand {\n    /// Create a new UnravelStrand.\n    ///\n    /// `state_dir` is the base directory for unravel state files\n    /// (e.g., `~/.needle/state/unravel/`).\n    pub fn new(\n        config: UnravelConfig,\n        workspace: PathBuf,\n        state_dir: PathBuf,\n        agent: Box<dyn UnravelAgent>,\n        telemetry: Telemetry,\n    ) -> Self {\n        UnravelStrand {\n            config,\n            workspace,\n            state_dir,\n            agent,\n            telemetry,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Compute the state file path for a workspace.\n    fn state_file_path(&self) -> PathBuf {\n        let hash = workspace_hash(&self.workspace);\n        self.state_dir.join(format!(\"{hash}.json\"))\n    }\n\n    /// Find beads with the \"human\" label from all beads in the store.\n    fn filter_human_beads(beads: &[Bead]) -> Vec<&Bead> {\n        beads\n            .iter()\n            .filter(|b| b.labels.iter().any(|l| l == \"human\"))\n            .collect()\n    }\n\n    /// Get existing child beads (unravel-proposal children) for a parent bead.\n    fn count_existing_children(bead: &Bead) -> u32 {\n        bead.dependencies\n            .iter()\n            .filter(|d| d.dependency_type == \"blocks\")\n            .count() as u32\n    }\n\n    /// Build the unravel prompt for a human-blocked bead.\n    ///\n    /// Uses `config.prompt_template` when set; otherwise falls back to the\n    /// built-in template.  Template variables: `{id}`, `{title}`, `{body}`,\n    /// `{labels}`.\n    fn build_prompt(&self, bead: &Bead) -> String {\n        let body = bead.body.as_deref().unwrap_or(\"(no description)\");\n        let labels = bead.labels.join(\", \");\n\n        if let Some(template) = &self.config.prompt_template {\n            return template\n                .replace(\"{id}\", bead.id.as_ref())\n                .replace(\"{title}\", &bead.title)\n                .replace(\"{body}\", body)\n                .replace(\"{labels}\", &labels);\n        }\n\n        format!(\n            \"## Human-Blocked Bead\\n\\n\\\n             **ID:** {id}\\n\\\n             **Title:** {title}\\n\\\n             **Description:**\\n\\\n             {body}\\n\\\n             **Labels:** {labels}\\n\\n\\\n             ## Task\\n\\n\\\n             This bead is blocked because it requires a human decision. \\\n             Propose automated alternatives that could accomplish the same \\\n             goal without human intervention.\\n\\n\\\n             For each alternative, provide:\\n\\\n             - **title**: concise description of the alternative approach\\n\\\n             - **body**: what needs to be done (actionable, an agent can execute it)\\n\\n\\\n             Constraints:\\n\\\n             - Alternatives must be fully automatable (no human decisions needed)\\n\\\n             - Each alternative should be a self-contained, actionable task\\n\\\n             - Propose only viable alternatives, not partial workarounds\\n\\n\\\n             Output a JSON array of objects with \\\"title\\\" and \\\"body\\\" fields.\\n\\\n             If no viable automated alternative exists, respond with: NO_ALTERNATIVES\",\n            id = bead.id,\n            title = bead.title,\n            body = body,\n            labels = labels,\n        )\n    }\n","numLines":200,"startLine":1,"totalLines":1214}}}

{\n    /// Create a new WeaveStrand.\n    ///\n    /// `state_dir` is the base directory for weave state files\n    /// (e.g., `~/.needle/state/weave/`).\n    pub fn new(\n        config: WeaveConfig,\n        workspace: PathBuf,\n        state_dir: PathBuf,\n        agent: Box<dyn WeaveAgent>,\n        telemetry: Telemetry,\n    ) -> Self {\n        WeaveStrand {\n            config,\n            workspace,\n            state_dir,\n            agent,\n            telemetry,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Compute the state file path for a workspace.\n    ///\n    /// Uses a SHA-256 hash of the workspace path to create a unique filename.\n    fn state_file_path(&self) -> PathBuf {\n        let hash = workspace_hash(&self.workspace);\n        self.state_dir.join(format!(\"{hash}.json\"))\n    }\n\n    /// Check if this workspace is excluded from weave.\n    fn is_workspace_excluded(&self) -> bool {\n        self.config\n            .exclude_workspaces\n            .iter()\n            .any(|excluded| excluded == &self.workspace)\n    }\n\n    /// Discover documentation files in the workspace using configured patterns.\n    fn discover_doc_files(&self) -> Vec<PathBuf> {\n        let mut files = Vec::new();\n        for pattern in &self.config.doc_patterns {\n            let full_pattern = self.workspace.join(pattern).display().to_string();\n            if let Ok(entries) = glob::glob(&full_pattern) {\n                for entry in entries.flatten() {\n                    if entry.is_file() && !files.contains(&entry) {\n                        files.push(entry);\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n        }\n        files.sort();\n        files\n    }\n\n    /// Format documentation files into a string for the prompt.\n    fn format_doc_files(files: &[PathBuf], workspace: &Path) -> String {\n        if files.is_empty() {\n            return \"(no documentation files found)\".to_string();\n        }\n\n        let mut sections = Vec::new();\n        for file in files {\n            let rel_path = file\n                .strip_prefix(workspace)\n                .unwrap_or(file)\n                .display()\n                .to_string();\n            match std::fs::read_to_string(file) {\n                Ok(content) => {\n                    sections.push(format!(\"### {rel_path}\\n\\n{}\", content.trim_end()));\n                }\n                Err(_) => {\n                    sections.push(format!(\"### {rel_path}\\n\\n(failed to read)\"));\n                }\n            }\n        }\n        sections.join(\"\\n\\n\")\n    }\n\n    /// Format existing beads into a string for the prompt.\n    fn format_existing_beads(beads: &[crate::types::Bead]) -> String {\n        if beads.is_empty() {\n            return \"(no open beads)\".to_string();\n        }","numLines":200,"startLine":1,"totalLines":1219}}}

\\\"priority\\\" fields.\\n\\\n285\t             Do not propose beads that duplicate any existing open beads listed above.\\n\\\n286\t             If no gaps are found, respond with: NO_GAPS\"\n287\t        )\n288\t    }\n289\t}\n290\t\n291\t/// Extract a JSON block from markdown-fenced content.\n292\tfn extract_json_block(text: &str) -> Option<&str> {\n293\t    // Look for ```json ... ``` or ``` ... ```\n294\t    let start_markers = [\"```json\\n\", \"```json\\r\\n\", \"```\\n\", \"```\\r\\n\"];\n295\t    for marker in &start_markers {\n296\t        if let Some(start) = text.find(marker) parse_agent_response(response: &str) -> Result<Vec<ProposedBead>> {\n        let trimmed = response.trim();\n\n        // Check for NO_GAPS sentinel.\n        if trimmed.contains(\"NO_GAPS\") {\n            return Ok(vec![]);\n        }\n\n        // Try parsing as a JSON array directly.\n        if let Ok(beads) = serde_json::from_str::<Vec<ProposedBead>>(trimmed) {\n            return       .iter()\n                    .filter_map(|v| serde_json::from_value(v.clone()).ok())\n                    .collect();\n                if !beads.is_empty() {\n                    return text[content_start..].find(\"```\") {\n                return Some(&text[content_start..content_start + end]);\n            }\n        }\n    }\n    None\n}\n\n/// Compute a short SHA-256 hash of a self.seen_fingerprints.insert(fingerprint.to_string());\n110\t    }\n111\t\n112\t    /// Update the last run timestamp.\n113\t    fn touch(&mut self) {\n114\t        self.last_run = Pulse strand: codebase health scans.\n//!\n//! Runs configured scanners (linters, test coverage, etc.) and creates beads\n//! for significant findings that exceed severity thresholds.\n//!\n//! **Guardrails:**\n//! - Opt-in only (disabled by default).\n//! - Max beads created per run.\n//! - 48-hour cooldown between runs per workspace.\n//! - Severity threshold filters minor issues.\n//! - Deduplication: issues already tracked don't create duplicate beads.\n//!\n//! **State:**\n//! - `~/.needle/state/pulse/<workspace-hash>.json` stores:\n//!   - Last run timestamp\n//!   - Set of seen issue fingerprints\n//!\n//! Depends on: `bead_store`, `config`, `telemetry`, `types`.\n\nuse std::collections::HashSet;\nuse std::path::{Path, PathBuf};\n\nuse anyhow::{Context, Result};\nuse chrono::{DateTime, Utc};\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse sha2::{Digest, Sha256};\n\nuse crate::bead_store::BeadStore;\nuse crate::config::PulseConfig;\nuse crate::telemetry::{EventKind, Telemetry};\nuse crate::types::StrandResult;\n\n// ─── Scanner finding (parsed from scanner output) ─────────────────────────────\n\n/// A single finding from a scanner.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct ScannerFinding {\n    /// Human-readable title for the finding.\n    pub title: String,\n    /// Detailed description of the issue.\n    pub body: String,\n    /// Severity level (1=critical, 5=minor).\n    pub severity: u8,\n    /// File path if applicable.\n    #[serde(default)]\n    pub file_path: Option<String>,\n    /// Line number if applicable.\n    #[serde(default)]\n    pub line: Option<u32>,\n    /// Unique fingerprint for deduplication.\n    pub fingerprint: String,\n}\n\n// ─── Persistent state ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Persisted state for the Pulse strand.\n///\n/// Tracks last run time and seen issue fingerprints for deduplication.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct PulseState {\n    /// Last time a pulse scan was run for this workspace.\n    pub last_run: Option<DateTime<Utc>>,\n    /// Set of issue fingerprints already seen (for dedup).\n    pub seen_fingerprints: HashSet<String>,\n}\n\nimpl PulseState {\n    /// Load state from disk, returning default if file doesn't exist.\n    fn load(path: &Path) -> Result<Self> {\n        match std::fs::read_to_string(path) {\n            Ok(data) => serde_json::from_str(&data)\n                .with_context(|| format!(\"failed to parse pulse state: {}\", path.display())),\n            Err(e) if e.kind() == std::io::ErrorKind::NotFound => Ok(Self::default()),\n            Err(e) => {\n                Err(e).with_context(|| format!(\"failed to read pulse state: {}\", path.display()))\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Persist state to disk.\n    fn save(&self, path: &Path) -> Result<()> {\n        if let Some(parent) = path.parent() {\n            std::fs::create_dir_all(parent)\n                .with_context(|| format!(\"failed to create state dir: {}\", parent.display()))?;\n        }\n        let data = serde_json::to_string_pretty(self).context(\"failed to serialize pulse state\")?;\n        std::fs::write(path, data)\n            .with_context(|| format!(\"failed to write pulse state: {}\", path.display()))\n    }\n\n    /// Check if cooldown has elapsed since last run.\n    fn is_in_cooldown(&self, cooldown_hours: i64) -> bool {\n        match self.last_run {\n            None => false,\n            Some(last) => {\n                let elapsed = Utc::now().signed_duration_since(last);\n                elapsed.num_hours() < cooldown_hours\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Check if a finding has already been seen.\n    fn has_seen(&self, fingerprint: &str) -> bool {\n        self.seen_fingerprints.contains(fingerprint)\n    }\n\n    /// Mark a finding as seen.\n    fn mark_seen(&mut self, fingerprint: &str) {\n        self.seen_fingerprints.insert(fingerprint.to_string());\n    }\n\n    /// Update the last run timestamp.\n    fn touch(&mut self) {\n        self.last_run = Some(Utc::now());\n    }\n}\n\n// ─── PulseStrand ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// The Pulse strand — runs health scanners and creates beads for return StrandResult::NoWork;\n        }\n\n        tracing::info!(\n            human_beads = human_beads.len(),\n            \"unravel strand: found human-labeled beads\"\n        );\n\n        // Process beads with guardrails.\n        let mut total_created = 0u32;\n        let mut beads_processed = 0u32;\n        let cooldown_hours = self.config.cooldown_hours as i64;\n\n        for bead in &human_beads {\n            // Guard: max beads per run.\n            if beads_processed >= self.config.max_beads_per_run {\n                tracing::info!(\n                    max = self.config.max_beads_per_run,\n                    \"unravel strand: max beads per run reached\"\n                );\n                break;\n            }\n\n            // Guard: cooldown — skip recently analyzed beads.\n            if state.is_in_cooldown(&bead.id, cooldown_hours) {\n                tracing::debug!(\n                    bead_id = %bead.id,\n                    \"unravel strand: bead is in cooldown, skipping\"\n                );\n                self.telemetry\n                    .emit(EventKind::UnravelSkipped {\n                        bead_id: bead.id.clone(),\n                        reason: \"cooldown\".to_string(),\n                    })\n                    .ok();\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            // Guard: max alternatives per bead — check existing children.\n            let existing_children = Self::count_existing_children(bead);\n            if existing_children >= self.config.max_alternatives_per_bead {\n                tracing::debug!(\n                    bead_id = %bead.id,\n                    existing_children,\n                    max = self.config.max_alternatives_per_bead,\n                    \"unravel strand: bead already has max alternatives, skipping\"\n                );\n                state.mark_analyzed(&bead.id);\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            let remaining_slots = self.config.max_alternatives_per_bead - existing_children;\n            beads_processed += 1;\n\n            // Build prompt and dispatch agent.\n            let prompt = self.build_prompt(bead);\n            let response = match self\n                .agent\n                .propose_alternatives(&prompt, &self.workspace)\n                .await\n            {\n                Ok(r) => r,\n                Err(e) => {\n                    tracing::warn!(\n                        bead_id = %bead.id,\n                        error = %e,\n                        \"unravel strand: agent dispatch failed\"\n                    );\n                    state.mark_analyzed(&bead.id);\n                    continue;\n                }\n            };\n\n            // Parse proposed alternatives.\n            let proposed = match Self::parse_agent_response(&response) {\n                Ok(p) => p,\n                Err(e) => {\n                    tracing::warn!(\n                        bead_id = %bead.id,\n                        error = %e,\n                        \"unravel strand: failed to parse agent response\"\n                    );\n                    state.mark_analyzed(&bead.id);\n                    continue;\n                }\n            };\n\n            if proposed.is_empty() {\n                tracing::info!(\n                    bead_id = %bead.id,\n                    \"unravel strand: no alternatives proposed\"\n                );\n                state.mark_analyzed(&bead.id);\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            // Create child beads as alternatives.\n            let mut created_for_this_bead = 0u32;\n            for alternative in &proposed {\n                // Guard: remaining slots for this bead.\n                if created_for_this_bead >= remaining_slots {\n                    break;\n                }\n\n                // Guard: max total created per run.\n                if total_created >= self.config.max_beads_per_run {\n                    break;\n                }\n\n                let child_title = format!(\"[Unravel] {} — {}\", bead.title, alternative.title);\n                let child_body = format!(\n                    \"## Alternative for: {}\\n\\\n                     Original bead: {id}\\n\\n\\\n                     {alt_body}\\n\\n\\\n                     ---\\n\\\n                     This is an automated alternative proposal created by the \\\n                     unravel strand. It is a child of the original human-blocked \\\n                     bead and can be executed independently.\",\n                    bead.title,\n                    id = bead.id,\n                    alt_body = alternative.body,\n                );\n                let labels: Vec<&str> = vec![\"unravel-proposal\"];\n\n                match store.create_bead(&child_title, &child_body, &labels).await {\n                    Ok(child_id) => {\n                        // Link child as blocking parent (child blocks original).\n                        if let Err(e) = store.add_dependency(&child_id, &bead.id).await {\n                            tracing::warn!(\n                                bead_id = %bead.id,\n                                child_id = %child_id,\n                                error = %e,\n                                \"unravel strand: failed to add dependency\"\n                            );\n                        } else {\n                            tracing::info!(\n                                parent_id = %bead.id,\n                                child_id = %child_id,\n                                child_title = %child_title,\n                                \"unravel strand: created alternative child bead\"\n                            );\n                        }\n\n                        created_for_this_bead += 1;\n                        total_created += 1;\n                    }\n                    Err(e) => {\n                        tracing::warn!(\n                            bead_id = %bead.id,\n                            error = %e,\n                            title = %alternative.title,\n                            \"unravel strand: failed to create child bead\"\n                        );\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n\n            // Emit telemetry for this bead.\n            self.telemetry\n                .emit(EventKind::UnravelAnalyzed {\n                    bead_id: bead.id.clone(),\n                    alternatives_proposed: created_for_this_bead,\n                })\n                .ok();\n\n            state.mark_analyzed(&bead.id);\n        }\n\n        // Save state.\n        if let Err(e) = state.save(&state_path) {\n            tracing::warn!(error = %e, \"unravel strand: failed to save state\");\n        }\n\n        if total_created > 0 {\n            tracing::info!(\n                created = total_created,\n                \"unravel strand: created alternative beads for human-blocked beads\"\n            );\n            StrandResult::WorkCreated\n        } else {\n            let _ = self.telemetry.emit(EventKind::StrandSkipped {\n                strand_name: \"unravel\".to_string(),\n                reason: \"no_new_alternatives_created\".to_string(),\n            });\n            StrandResult::NoWork\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n// ─── Production agent ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Production `UnravelAgent` that invokes the configured AI agent via subprocess.\n///\n/// The agent binary is called with `--print` so it emits the response as plain\n/// text on stdout without tool-use side-effects.  The prompt is written to a\n/// temp file and fed via stdin redirection.\npub struct CliUnravelAgent {\n    /// Agent binary name or path (e.g., `\"claude\"`).\n    agent_cmd: String,\n}\n\nimpl CliUnravelAgent {\n    /// Create a new `CliUnravelAgent`.\n    ///\n    /// `agent_cmd` is the binary used for analysis (typically taken from\n    /// `config.agent.default`).\n    pub fn new(agent_cmd: String) -> Self {\n        CliUnravelAgent { agent_cmd }\n    }\n}\n\n#[async_trait::async_trait]\nimpl UnravelAgent for CliUnravelAgent {\n    async fn propose_alternatives(&self, prompt: &str, workspace: &Path) -> Result<String> {\n        // Write the prompt to a temp file.\n        let tmp_dir = std::env::temp_dir().join(\"needle\");\n        std::fs::create_dir_all(&tmp_dir)","numLines":300,"startLine":200,"totalLines":1214}}}

Structured telemetry — JSONL event stream, never on stdout/stderr.\n//!\n//! Every state transition, claim attempt, dispatch, and outcome emits a typed\n//! event. The emitter is non-blocking: events are queued and written by a\n//! background task. A broken sink never blocks or panics the worker.\n//!\n//! ## Architecture\n//! ```text\n//! worker → emit() → mpsc::Sender → [background task] → TelemetryBus → Vec<Box<dyn Sink>>\n//! ```\n//!\n//! Depends on: `types`.\n\nuse std::path::{Path, PathBuf};\nuse std::sync::atomic::{AtomicU64, Ordering};\nuse std::sync::Arc;\n\nuse anyhow::{Context, Result};\nuse chrono::{DateTime, Utc};\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse tokio::sync::mpsc;\n\nuse crate::config::{ColorMode, HookConfig, StdoutFormat, StdoutSinkConfig, TelemetryConfig};\nuse crate::types::{BeadId, WorkerId, WorkerState};\n\n// ─── TelemetryEvent ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// A single structured telemetry record.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct TelemetryEvent {\n    /// UTC timestamp with millisecond precision.\n    pub timestamp: DateTime<Utc>,\n    /// Discriminant string, e.g. `\"state_transition\"`.\n    pub event_type: String,\n    /// The worker that emitted this event.\n    pub worker_id: WorkerId,\n    /// Session identifier (8 hex chars, unique per worker boot).\n    pub session_id: String,\n    /// Monotonically increasing counter within the session.\n    pub sequence: u64,\n    /// Bead context when relevant.\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub bead_id: Option<BeadId>,\n    /// Workspace directory when relevant.\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub workspace: Option<PathBuf>,\n    /// Event-specific payload as a JSON value.\n    pub data: serde_json::Value,\n    /// Optional duration in milliseconds for timed operations.\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub duration_ms: Option<u64>,\n    /// W3C trace-id hex (32 lowercase hex chars). Present only when emitted inside an OTel span.\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub trace_id: Option<String>,\n    /// W3C span-id hex (16 lowercase hex chars). Present only when emitted inside an OTel span.\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub span_id: Option<String>,\n}\n\n// ─── EventKind ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Typed event variants emitted by all NEEDLE components.\n///\n/// Every variant maps to a `TelemetryEvent` with `event_type` matching\n/// the snake_case discriminant name.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n#[non_exhaustive]\npub enum EventKind {\n    // ── Worker lifecycle ──\n    WorkerBooting {\n        worker_name: String,\n        version: String,\n    },\n    WorkerStarted {\n        worker_name: String,\n        version: String,\n    },\n    WorkerStopped {\n        reason: String,\n        beads_processed: u64,\n        uptime_secs: u64,\n    },\n    WorkerErrored {\n        error_type: String,\n        error_message: String,\n        beads_processed: u64,\n    },\n    WorkerExhausted {\n        cycle_count: u64,\n        last_strand: String,\n        /// How many times the waterfall restarted before exhausting.\n        waterfall_restarts: u32,\n        /// Name of each strand that returned WorkCreated (one entry per restart).\n        restart_triggers: Vec<String>,\n    },\n    WorkerIdle {\n        backoff_seconds: u64,\n    },\n    StateTransition {\n        from: WorkerState,\n        to: WorkerState,\n    },\n    InitStepStarted {\n        step: String,\n    },\n    InitStepCompleted {\n        step: String,\n        duration_ms: u64,\n    },\n    WorkerBootTimeout {\n        elapsed_ms: u64,\n    },\n\n    // ── Strand evaluation ──\n    StrandEvaluated {\n        strand_name: String,\n        result: String,\n        duration_ms: u64,\n    },\n    StrandSkipped {\n        strand_name: String,\n        reason: String,\n    },\n    QueueEmpty,\n\n    // ── Bead claim ──\n    ClaimAttempt {\n        bead_id: BeadId,\n        attempt: u32,\n    },\n    ClaimSuccess {\n        bead_id: BeadId,\n    },\n    ClaimRaceLost {\n        bead_id: BeadId,\n    },\n    ClaimFailed {\n        bead_id: BeadId,\n        reason: String,\n    },\n    ClaimRaceLostSkipped {\n Worker loop: the core NEEDLE state machine.\n//!\n//! The Worker executes the strand waterfall, claims beads, dispatches the\n//! agent, handles outcomes, and emits telemetry for every transition.\n//!\n//! State transitions are explicit — there is no implicit fallthrough and no\n//! state that does not have a defined handler. The worker emits telemetry for\n//! every transition.\n//!\n//! Depends on: `strand`, `claim`, `prompt`, `dispatch`, `outcome`,\n//!             `bead_store`, `telemetry`, `health`, `config`, `types`.\n\nuse std::collections::HashSet;\nuse std::path::PathBuf;\nuse std::sync::atomic::{AtomicBool, Ordering};\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse std::time::Instant;\n\nuse anyhow::{bail, Context, Result};\n\n#[cfg(unix)]\nuse std::sync::atomic::{AtomicU32, AtomicUsize, Ordering as AtomicOrdering};\n\nuse crate::bead_store::BeadStore;\nuse crate::canary::CanaryRunner;\nuse crate::claim::Claimer;\nuse crate::commit_hook;\nuse crate::config::{Config, ConfigLoader};\nuse crate::cost::{self, BudgetCheck, EffortData};\nuse crate::dispatch::{self, Dispatcher};\nuse crate::health::HealthMonitor;\nuse crate::mitosis::MitosisEvaluator;\nuse crate::outcome::OutcomeHandler;\nuse crate::prompt::{BuiltPrompt, PromptBuilder};\nuse crate::rate_limit::RateLimiter;\nuse crate::registry::{Registry, WorkerEntry};\nuse crate::strand::StrandRunner;\nuse crate::telemetry::{EventKind, Telemetry};\nuse crate::types::{AgentOutcome, Bead, BeadId, ClaimResult, IdleAction, Outcome, WorkerState};\nuse crate::upgrade::{self, HotReloadCheck};\n\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// Global shutdown flag for signal handlers\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Global pointer to the shutdown flag, used by synchronous signal handlers.\n/// This is necessary because signal handlers run in a separate context and\n/// cannot easily access the Worker's shutdown flag directly.\n#[cfg(unix)]\nstatic GLOBAL_SHUTDOWN_FLAG: AtomicUsize = AtomicUsize::new(0);\n\n/// Set the global shutdown flag pointer.\n/// Called by `install_signal_handlers` to register the shutdown flag.\n#[cfg(unix)]\nfn set_global_shutdown_flag(ptr: usize) {\n    GLOBAL_SHUTDOWN_FLAG.store(ptr, AtomicOrdering::SeqCst);\n}\n\n/// Clear the global shutdown flag pointer.\n/// Called when the worker is dropped to avoid dangling pointers.\n#[cfg(unix)]\nfn clear_global_shutdown_flag() {\n    GLOBAL_SHUTDOWN_FLAG.store(0, AtomicOrdering::SeqCst);\n    LAST_SIGNAL.store(0, AtomicOrdering::SeqCst);\n}\n\n/// Track the last received signal for diagnostic logging.\n/// AtomicU32 allows lock-free reads/writes from the signal handler.\n#[cfg(unix)]\nstatic LAST_SIGNAL: AtomicU32 = AtomicU32::new(0);\n\n/// Synchronous signal handler for SIGTERM, SIGINT, and SIGHUP.\n///\n/// This function is called directly by the OS when a signal is received.\n/// It must be async-signal-safe: no allocation, no locking, no I/O.\n/// We set the atomic shutdown flag, record the signal number, and return immediately.\n#[cfg(unix)]\nextern \"C\" fn signal_handler(sig: i32) {\n    // SAFETY: The signal handler is only installed after set_global_shutdown_flag\n    // has been called with a valid pointer. The pointer remains valid for the\n    // entire lifetime of the worker process.\n    let ptr = GLOBAL_SHUTDOWN_FLAG.load(AtomicOrdering::SeqCst) as *const AtomicBool;\n    if !ptr.is_null() {\n        // SAFETY: The pointer is valid and points to an AtomicBool that lives\n        // for the entire program duration.\n        unsafe {\n            (*ptr).store(true, AtomicOrdering::SeqCst);\n        }\n        // Record the signal number so the main loop can log it.\n        LAST_SIGNAL.store(sig as u32, AtomicOrdering::SeqCst);\n    }\n}\n\n/// Install synchronous signal handlers for SIGTERM, SIGINT, and SIGHUP.\n///\n/// Uses libc::sigaction to register handlers that set the shutdown flag\n/// immediately when a signal is received. This ensures that signals are\n/// caught even if the tokio runtime hasn't polled async signal tasks yet.\n#[cfg(unix)]\nunsafe fn install_unix_signal_handlers() {\n    use libc::{sigaction, sigemptyset, SA_RESTART, SIGHUP, SIGINT, SIGTERM};\n\n    // Set up the sigaction structure.\n    let mut act: libc::sigaction = std::mem::zeroed();\n    act.sa_sigaction = signal_handler as *const () as usize;\n    // Block all signals during handler execution to prevent re-entrancy issues.\n    sigemptyset(&mut act.sa_mask as *mut libc::sigset_t);\n    // Use SA_RESTART to automatically restart system calls interrupted by signals.\n    act.sa_flags = SA_RESTART;\n\n    // Install handlers for SIGTERM, SIGINT, and SIGHUP.\n    // We ignore errors here - if a handler can't be installed, we'll log a\n    // warning but continue. The async handlers (below) provide a fallback.\n    for &sig in &[SIGTERM, SIGINT, SIGHUP] {\n        let mut old: libc::sigaction = std::mem::zeroed();\n        if sigaction(sig, &act, &mut old) == 0 {\n            tracing::debug!(signal = sig, \"installed synchronous signal handler\");\n        } else {\n            // Log the error but don't fail - the async handlers provide a fallback.\n            tracing::warn!(\n                signal = sig,\n                errno = *libc::__errno_location(),\n                \"failed to install synchronous signal handler\"\n            );\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Stub implementations for non-Unix platforms.\n/// These functions are no-ops on platforms where Unix signals are not available.\n#[cfg(not(unix))]\nfn set_global_shutdown_flag(_ptr: usize) {\n    // No-op on non-Unix platforms\n}\n\n#[cfg(not(unix))]\nfn clear_global_shutdown_flag() {\n    // No-op on non-Unix platforms\n}\n\n/// The NEEDLE worker — owns and drives the full state machine.\npub struct Worker {\n    config: Config,\n    worker_name: String,\n    store: Arc<dyn BeadStore>,\n    /// Home workspace store — kept for restore after processing a remote bead.\n    home_store: Arc<dyn BeadStore>,\n    telemetry: Telemetry,\n    strands: StrandRunner,\n    claimer: Claimer,","numLines":150,"startLine":1,"totalLines":2939}}}
